author: @stephendudley4377
text: Hey Mr Robert, sorry to say but I was listening to this video while working and heard you say a ping pong ball will fit into a 3/4 inch coupling? Respectfully, no sir I believe your conversions were incorrect, I apologize for just now pointing it out but I got busy and forgot, it'll take about twice a 3/4 inch fitting for a ping pong ball, it will fit very nicely in a 1 1/2 inch pvc coupling which here in the states are fairly standard for sink drains and such. So anyone reading this please adjust accordingly if you plan on using this truly ingenious creation me Robert has come up with, 1 1/2 inch pipe and fittings, or just use some reducers if you have already gotten a bunch of 1 1/2 inch pipe, I believe that wouldn't hurt anything. Thank you for your awesome content Mr Robert, it's truly appreciated, if I had more equipment than I do at the present time I would make most if not all of your inventions/improvements on existing Inventions, sadly, though I do have a fair amount of tools, they're all slanted towards the woodworking side. Table saw, miter saws, and routers and such, also a decent Lincoln mig welder. So a lot of them I've seen are out m y reach, for now at least. I'm dying to get my hands on a decent 3d printer but alas I'm but a humble carpenter who works at a mid sized college in my home town, the great majority of my funds go to supporting my family, we're lucky to make our house and grocery bills in these terrible times, brought about deliberately to try to implement an agenda, of course, but nevertheless trying times indeed they are. Have a wonderful day to all, thank you again for your wonderful ideas my Robert

text: Greetings Greetings... my names Patrick from south Africa the load shedding is bad in this country up to 12 hours a day in some places... poor people need a cheap & efficient  form of energy generation... I know what you are doing can make a world of difference in many lives... what would you recommend as a cheap & easy to make wind turbine that could be used by the average low income household to generate 500/600watts of usable energy

author: @Thunderstormworld
text: Many years ago I came across a brass bellow and thought of how you could make a solar water pump. The operation would be as the sun heats up the brass bellow it expands and the piston pump side pumps the water out that flows out over the brass bellow which will cool it and so the cycle continues .

author: @danieldewindt3919
text: Thank you for sharing! :)

author: @austinwebdev
text: Thank you!!!! This gave me an idea. Instead of the magic gear, I'll use a bicycle tire pump and a vane motor connected to a small air tank to act as a pneumatic battery. I'm going to put it between the floor and the leaf springs of my RV. I'm trying to harness some of the power of the rocking and swaying when I move around inside or drive over a bump.

author: @NoOneButRonPaul
text: Hello. Thank you so much for your videos. I've piped in in 800m of 25mm hose from an 85m height and this is exactly what i'm looking for. I'm getting water from a natural spring. I even have a 3d printer waiting to go. Is there an easy way to calculate a size of this in order to achieve a constant 12v? Or even 24v as my solar system is 24v. I have no idea how much head I'll be getting but I was looking to splitting the flow and turning one outlet into a pumping irrigation system and fill my tanks. All in one video. Wow.

author: @lorenedailey8691
text: When the sun don't shine and the wind is at rest you want the biggest bellows you can make. Not to pump it, but to put a weight on top. And have the bellows exhale as slowly as posible. The air outlet should be a nozzle. Just set it and forget it. When the air is all expelled set it again. It would be good if the system took at least an hour to cycle.

author: @dr.froghopper6711

replies.author: @bearnaff9387
replies.text:
I'm glad I'm not the only one who comes back to hear the cackling.

It's like he's in on some major fault or loophole in the laws of physics, and each of these videos are part of a long chain where he's slowly verifying the extent and degree of the fault.  He laughs because each little video brings him one step closer to to being ready to exploit the loophole.  He laughs because each video shows God that Heaven's loss is as inevitable as the dawn.

author: @philipvecchio3292
text:
Probably 10 years ago I bought a device that was supposed to charge your phone or run a flashlight. It would take forever to charge your phone with this little crank.  I wonder if this would work better than that.

One great application would be for a bicyclist.  They generally already have a bike Pump, a small valve section and generator could be an easy way to charge portable electronics remotely, especially when bicycling cross country.

replies.author: @TnTOmnibus
replies.text: energy out is always less than or equal to energy in mate - if your phone needs 20 watts that's about half an hour of cranking whatever you use as arm power  is in the region of 40 - 60 watts per hour

replies.author: @philipvecchio3292
replies.text: @TnTOmnibus Maybe with a bike pump it's more ergonomic than a little hand Crank.

replies.author: @TnTOmnibus
replies.text: good point mate - i would think it is

replies.author: @VeniceInventors
replies.text:
You just need an acoustic generator! The more you talk on the phone, the more charge it gets 🙂
Of course it wouldn't be enough to keep up with the drain from the CPU and radio.
Which reminds me of the first AM radio I built, which didn't need a battery as it was powered by the AM signal itself, surprisingly getting just enough power to play the sound out of an earpiece. 
And now I'm wondering if the opposite would work too, that is, powering an AM transmitter with a microphone. As long as the range is greater than yelling, battery-less CBs would be cool.
